title: phlox × frondosa
intro: Phlox × frondosa, also known as round-leaved phlox, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its fragrant flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The medicinal properties of Phlox × frondosa lie in its ability to act as a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, as well as its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Phlox × frondosa can be made into infusions, decoctions, or salves, and are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments, including colds, flu, and skin irritations.
